description: 15 named points of interest on the estate with GPS coordinates

hotspots.name: Le Château
hotspots.description: Louis XIII-style château — 440 m² per floor, 4.7m ceilings, 9 bedrooms, medieval cellars

hotspots.name: L'Orangerie
hotspots.description: Family apartment in a separate manor house — 3 bedrooms, garden terrace

hotspots.name: Le Pigeonnier
hotspots.description: Intimate tower retreat — converted 17th-century dovecote for couples

hotspots.name: Les Écuries
hotspots.description: U-shaped stable block with garages — event space and workshop studio

hotspots.name: Maison du Gardien
hotspots.description: The caretaker's house at the estate entrance

hotspots.name: La Piscine
hotspots.description: Heated pool (15 × 7 m) overlooking the orchard

hotspots.name: Le Sauna Finlandais
hotspots.description: Wood-fired Finnish sauna — complimentary for guests

hotspots.name: L'Étang Supérieur
hotspots.description: Upper pond — spring-fed by the stream crossing the park, fringed with willows

hotspots.name: L'Étang Inférieur
hotspots.description: Lower pond — cascading from the upper, home to herons and kingfishers

hotspots.name: La Forêt
hotspots.description: 30+ hectares of ancient oak, chestnut, and beech woodland with walking trails

hotspots.name: Le Jardin Formel
hotspots.description: English-style walled park with formal gardens, great trees, and vast allées

hotspots.name: Le Potager
hotspots.description: Kitchen garden — organic herbs, vegetables, seasonal fruit

hotspots.name: La Glacière
hotspots.description: Historic ice house — rare surviving example from the estate's early period

hotspots.name: La Serre
hotspots.description: Original greenhouse — heritage structure within the walled garden

hotspots.name: La Grande Allée
hotspots.description: Long paved avenue from the entrance gate to the château — lined with ancient trees
